title: NA-Non ULIP
word_text:
SEO Insertion on Here Is How You Can Check The Details of Your Existing Endowment Insurance Plans - Article Page
Keyword density and no. of target keywords has been mentioned as comments in the beginning
Suggested additional anchor texts are marked as comments in Yellow. (If required)
Keywords: endowment policy, endowment plan, endowment savings plan
Body Content:
As people grow more aware of the need for preparing early to meet their expenses post retirement, different tools have emerged in the marketplace to meet these needs. It is important to begin planning early as it enables you to carefully assess the sort of expenses you can be expected to have once you retire and no longer have an active income. It helps you take care not only of your expenses but also ensure that your family continues to live comfortably once you have retired.
An endowment policy is one such tool that can help you save up for your expenses post retirement. The premiums you pay towards an endowment plan earn interest and you receive the payout at the end of the term period, providing you are alive at the end of the policy term.
Since the endowment savings plan is meant to ease your expenses after retirement, it is important to keep a keen eye on it. These days, with digitalisation of most processes, it has become significantly easier to keep track of most financial plans and tools.
Ways to Track the Details of Your Existing Endowment Insurance Plans
With an endowment policy, your insurer will offer multiple ways for you to access the details of your policy. Read on below to learn the different ways in which you can keep track of your endowment plan.
Reaching out to your Insurer:The easiest way to check on the details of your endowment plan is to visit your insurer and check with them. Just make sure you have your policy number with you and any other required documents in order to ensure a quickless and seamless procedure after having reached the insurer’s office.
Register on Insurer’s Online Portal:Most insurers these days have an online portal for customers. Through these portals, customers can stay updated on details of their endowment policy, including the premiums due, the maturity period, and the payout amount. It is usually a simple process once the customer has been issued an ID through which they can log in and access these details. Some insurers even let policyholders pay the premiums on their endowment savings plan online through these portals.
Call Customer Care:Calling customer care is a familiar process for most people and it is an essential team for most companies these days. Most insurers have customer care teams that are required to  respond promptly to the customers when they call. Policyholders can call up the insurer’s customer care number and provide their policy number and credentials to the team in order to check details of their policy.
Check Via SMS:Checking details of your endowment policy via SMS is one of the easiest ways to access details of your policy. Insurers usually offer a number on which policyholders can send a simple text from their registered mobile number to obtain details of their endowment savings plan. Various insurers offer this as a route to get different kinds of details, including the premiums due.
Conclusion
It is essential to keep a check on the details of an endowment policy since it could well be the best savings plan for meeting your post retirement needs. However, it is important to note that the benefits of an endowment plan are only available to the policyholder if they are alive till the maturity period of the policy. If not, the entire amount is forfeited.
As a result, it is often recommended to get a traditional life insurance policy along with opting for an endowment savings plan. With a life insurance policy, a policyholder can nominate their dependents as beneficiaries, who could then receive the sum assured in case the policyholder is no longer around. It is an extremely beneficial policy since it enables policyholders to ensure that the needs of their beneficiaries are well looked after even if they are no longer around. This is especially important when the policyholder is the breadwinner for the family and all the other members of the family are dependent on them.
It is best to start planning for a life insurance policy well in advance. It is better to avail a life insurance plan when you are younger since your financial responsibilities would be fewer and there might not be dependents whose expenses you need to cover. Younger people generally have higher disposable incomes, which can be diverted towards a life insurance policy, ensuring that it is one less thing to worry about as you age. Additionally, at a younger age, your health is expected to be better, which means your premiums would be lower. If you opt for the same life insurance policy later in your life, there is a good chance that the premiums would be significantly higher. To avail low premium costs, it’s best to avail a life insurance policy as soon as you begin working. Another significant advantage to start investing in a life insurance policy earlier in life is the power of compounding that plays an impact on the premiums you invest. The longer the policy term, the higher is the sum assured at the end of the maturity period. You could gather quite a significant corpus at the end of your policy term. In case you are not around by the end of the policy term, it will also give you mental peace and stability to know that your family and dependents will be well looked after even if you are no longer.
It is everyone’s dream to grow old with their family and friends around them, living long and healthy lives. However, this is something people don’t really have control over, and a life insurance policy is the single best tool to ensure that even if you are no longer around, your family will still be looked after. As the breadwinner for the family, you won’t have to worry about your family having to drop their standard of living or being unable to achieve any of their dreams just because you are not around.
That’s why, as soon as you begin hunting for the best endowment policy for yourself, make sure to complement it with the best life insurance plan as well.
